input-messages:
which commit did we have claude as the commiter? i do not want that and we need to make it very clear in agent vault and all the global agent .md or whatever .md they use that this should not be done. always have me as the commiter this is very important can we revert that some how
i see this on agent hotline though 
this is in hunt repo too so can we make sure the changes we added will affect every agent on this computer 
should the .claude and .codex even get commited though i dont hink i want them to be added to the repo or all future repos
last-assistant-message:
Yes, you’re right: `.claude/` and `.codex/` should not be committed for this repo or future repos.

I fixed `agent-hotline`:

- Removed tracked `.claude/` and `.codex/` files from Git without deleting your local copies
- Added `.claude/`, `.codex/`, `.gemini/`, `.agents/` to [agent-hotline .gitignore](C:/Users/sushi/Documents/Github/agent-hotline/.gitignore:17)
- Pushed commit `27902e0` to `master`
- Verified `origin/master` no longer contains `.claude/` or `.codex/`

I also made it computer-wide:

- Added global ignore file: [C:\Users\sushi\.gitignore_global](C:/Users/sushi/.gitignore_global:1)
- Set `core.excludesfile` to that file, so future repos ignore those folders by default
- Updated global/vault agent rules to say never commit local agent config dirs

Important nuance: global ignore prevents future accidental adds, but it does not automatically remove files already tracked in existing repos. Those need one-time `git rm --cached -r .claude .codex` cleanup per repo.

Your unrelated local changes in `agent-hotline` were left alone.

::git-commit{cwd="C:\Users\sushi\Documents\Github\agent-hotline"}
::git-push{cwd="C:\Users\sushi\Documents\Github\agent-hotline" branch="master"}
